originalBehavior: The explainer renders two movable event shelves over a shared sample space and keeps their overlap geometry visible while values change.
localStatus: The local route keeps the original waterfall geometry and overlap calculations unchanged, with only shell-level cleanup around the interactive.
notes: The page still uses the original custom waterfall directive and overlap math.
Relative EV asset paths were patched so the geometry renders under nested subpath hosting.
evidence: The implementation plan treats sample-space visualization and overlap manipulation as parity-critical.
Shared smoke coverage updates the sliders and waits for the rendered waterfall and bars to stay in sync.

originalBehavior: Readers can switch perspective between the full world, P(B|A), and P(A|B), which changes the visible sample-space window and interpretation text together.
localStatus: The local route keeps the original perspective buttons and domain zoom logic, including the conditional-lens transitions driven by the Angular watchers.
notes: The button labels and watcher logic were preserved exactly.
No footer or shell cleanup runs inside the interactive controls.
evidence: The parity target explicitly calls out the conditioning lens and reverse-conditioning comparison flow.
Smoke coverage toggles the perspective buttons and checks the active state changes.

originalBehavior: Slider changes update the displayed probabilities, conditional formulas, expected bars, and actual bars together.

notes: The formula image and value text remain in their original positions.

evidence: The current plan requires synchronized probability, table, and formula updates.
